question_title: Will the community prediction be higher than 14.00% on 2026-03-23 for the Metaculus question "Will Lisa Cook cease to be a member of the Federal Reserve Board of Governors before November 3, 2026?"?
summary: This meta-prediction asks whether the Metaculus CP on Lisa Cook's Fed tenure question will exceed 14.00% on March 23, 2026. The CP has oscillated tightly between 12-14% for 6+ weeks and has NEVER exceeded 14% — meaning 14% is the observed ceiling, not a midpoint. The threshold requires STRICTLY above 14.00%. The underlying fundamentals strongly favor Cook staying (SCOTUS appeared skeptical of Trump at oral arguments, courts have consistently blocked removal, no resignation signals), which should keep CP low or push it lower. The main pathway to YES is either random forecaster noise pushing CP slightly above the boundary (~21% contribution) or a very unlikely adverse SCOTUS ruling (~3% contribution). The scenario decomposition yields ~25% probability.

factors.description: CP has oscillated between 12-14% for 6+ weeks, NEVER exceeding 14%. The threshold is at the observed ceiling.
Supreme Court appeared skeptical of Trump's position at January oral arguments - likely to rule in Cook's favor, which would push CP DOWN, not up
Small probability (~3%) of SCOTUS ruling against Cook in the next 7 days, which would spike CP well above 14%
Random forecaster noise could push CP slightly above 14% even without a catalyst - CP is right at the boundary
124 forecasters makes the CP relatively stable - harder for noise to move it significantly
Recent news (judge quashing Powell subpoenas, Fed independence protected) all suggests Cook stays - should push CP down, not up

reasoning:
**Forecast: 0.25 (25%)**

Key reasoning:
- The CP has been in a very tight 12-14% range for 6+ weeks, **never exceeding 14%** — and the question requires *strictly above* 14.00%
- The Supreme Court appeared skeptical of Trump's attempt to fire Cook at January oral arguments — a ruling in Cook's favor (the likely outcome) would push CP *down*, not up
- Recent legal developments (judge quashing Powell subpoenas, Tillis blocking Warsh) all reinforce Fed independence, suggesting CP should decline
- The main YES pathway is random forecaster noise pushing CP marginally above the boundary (~21% contribution), with a small contribution from the unlikely scenario of an adverse SCOTUS ruling (~3%)
- 124 forecasters provides reasonable stability, making large random movements unlikely
sources_consulted: [(Metaculus) Will Lisa Cook cease to be a member of the Federal Reserve Board of Governors before November 3, 2026?](https://www.metaculus.com/questions/39711)
resolution_criteria: This question will resolve based on the community prediction of the Metaculus question found [here](https://www.metaculus.com/questions/39711) (i.e. the 'target question'). If the community prediction of the target question on 2026-03-23 22:02:23 is higher than 14.00%, this question will resolve to 'Yes'. If it is lower or equal to 14.00%, this question will resolve to 'No'. If the target question has already resolved before this question opens, then this question will be annulled. If the target question resolves after this question opens, but before 2026-03-23 22:02:23, then this question will resolve to the same value as the target question.
